title: antennaria dioica var. corymbosa
intro: Antennaria dioica var. corymbosa, commonly known as gray's leatherflower or mountain everlasting, is a plant species within the genus Antennaria, characterized by its unique, small, yellow or white flowers arranged in a corymbous inflorescence. It has been used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which help to soothe and calm irritated skin, wounds, and other injuries. The medicinal properties of Antennaria dioica var. corymbosa are attributed to its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and prevent infection, making it beneficial for treating various health conditions, including skin irritations, ulcers, and respiratory issues. Antennaria dioica var. corymbosa can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve to harness its medicinal benefits, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its therapeutic effects.
